questions.question: What type of chart is used to display the distribution of a single variable?
questions.explanation: A Histogram is a graphical representation of the distribution of a single variable.

questions.question: Which of the following types of plots is used to show the relationship between two variables?
questions.explanation: A Scatter Plot is used to show the relationship between two variables.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'ggplot2' package in R?
options: B) To create pie charts and other common statistical graphics
C) To create line plots and other common statistical graphics
D) To create bar plots and other common statistical graphics
questions.explanation: The 'ggplot2' package is used to create a wide range of common statistical graphics, including plots of lines, bars, scatter plots, and more.

questions.question: Which of the following types of plots is used to compare the distribution of two or more variables?
questions.explanation: A Box Plot is used to compare the distribution of two or more variables.

questions.question: What type of plot is used to display the relationship between two variables with different scales?
questions.explanation: A Scatter Plot can be used to display the relationship between two variables with different scales.
